title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, Volume III: source lineage
summary: CME00048 represents Fowler's 1901 volume III title page and printed pp. 577–743, an edited archival selection rather than one medieval work or manuscript. Current catalogs establish 233 exact public Durham IIIF objects (1571 canvases), modeled at physical-copy level; multi-bill manifests are separated from their physical bills, and face-only inventories remain partial. Exact non-public records and unresolved historical-to-current crosswalks are preserved without inventing sources.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00048
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00048.
TEMPHEAD's 2016 review history is a later proofing and structural pass, not an original-keying date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00048
entities.description: Public electronic text encoding the volume III title page and printed pp. 577–743. It omits the Surtees Society preliminaries, preface, contents, general index, glossary, subject lists, retrospective introduction, and society matter.

entities.label: Joseph Thomas Fowler, Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, volume III (1901)
entities.description: The concluding Surtees Society volume, a selective edition/calendar of Bursar and coal-mine accounts, post-Dissolution Treasurer's books, and miscellaneous bills, vouchers, rolls, and fragments. CME00048 encodes its title page and documentary text through p. 743, not the reference apparatus or retrospective introduction.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS., vol. III, Publications of the Surtees Society 103 (Durham: Andrews & Co. for the Society, 1901).
bibliographic.title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS.
bibliographic.publisher: Andrews & Co. for the Surtees Society
bibliographic.pages: Preliminaries; continuously paginated text pp. 577–989; retrospective introduction pp. i–lxvii

physical_description.extent: Title matter, pp. 577–989, retrospective introduction pp. i–lxvii, and society matter in a 524-page access PDF
physical_description.format: Printed scholarly calendar and edited documentary collection
notes: The Surtees council resolution calls this volume the first volume for the Society's year 1900; the title page, preface, and imprint are dated 1901.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Fowler volume III
entities.description: A 524-page PDF retaining the provider's complete exposed bound-volume sequence. CME00048's title page is PDF page 9 and its printed text pp. 577–743 is PDF pages 17–183.

Commercial reuse is outside the NonCommercial grant; adaptation is outside NoDerivatives. Obtain written permission before Git publication, a public viewer, print-on-demand, or other publication.

evidence.locator: Especially pp. i–ii, xxiii–xxx, and lxiii–lxvii

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hundreds of separate account rolls, duplicates, drafts, fragments, schedules, books, bills, and vouchers
editorial_practices.representation: A few records printed extensively, selected particulars extracted from others, and further sources mentioned or described
editorial_practices.description: Fowler's prefaces and the current catalog cross-references demonstrate a selective calendar rather than one diplomatic transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Current entries distinguish drafts, fair copies, counterparts, schedules, dorse texts, rejoined pieces, and aggregate bill groups
editorial_practices.representation: Edition headings and extracts arranged as a chronological documentary sequence
editorial_practices.description: The lineage retains exact current copies and IIIF ARKs rather than equating one Fowler DIV2 with one physical object.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete printed volume with title matter, preface, contents, pp. 577–989, reference apparatus, retrospective introduction, and society matter
editorial_practices.representation: The edition title page and printed pp. 577–743 only
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item stops before the general index, glossary, subject lists, and retrospective introduction.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Primarily Latin documentary prose with embedded Middle English and Fowler's modern English editorial prose
editorial_practices.description: Collation against the edition shows that the single inherited encoding label is not a reliable whole-item language analysis; metadata scopes Latin, Middle English, and editorial English separately.

editorial_practices.representation: TEMPHEAD records a later proofing and structural review
editorial_practices.description: The review note does not supply an original keying date or complete production history.

open_questions.question: How do all 248 Fowler/XML DIV2 units map many-to-many onto current physical records, copies, schedules, fragments, dorse texts, and bill groups?
next_steps: Extend the present exact catalog/ARK crosswalk to every editorial unit and sub-canvas passage.
Retain copy-specific SS103 versus SS100 clauses and do not force one DIV2 to one object.

open_questions.question: Do fuller surrogates exist for the 84 public objects whose manifests expose face-only or otherwise incomplete physical coverage?
next_steps: Send Durham the exact partial-coverage ARK list.
Ask whether omitted dorses or reverse sides are blank, inaccessible, or simply unpublished.

open_questions.question: Can complete physical-object facsimiles be obtained for the exact non-public office/mine copies, Treasurer's books, and identified Miscellaneous Charter bills and fragments?
next_steps: Request every surface and attachment of each exact object; request full current bound Treasurer's volumes including bindings and blanks.
For 7112, 7113, and 7119, first ask Durham to supply exact sub-item identifiers.

open_questions.question: Which current physical book corresponds to Fowler's imperfect Treasurer's book labelled 1563–4?
next_steps: Compare Fowler's extracts and physical description against DCD/L/BB/2 (1561–62), /3 (1562–63), and /4 (1564–65).
Do not silently assign the source to an adjacent current date.

open_questions.question: What are the current exact shelfmarks for the Recepta de ecclesiis compilation, John Oll's 1441 receipt account, the 1544 Durham Treasury travel manuscript, the c.1553 Treasurer's roll, the 1554 loose slip, and the framed 1541–42 notes?
next_steps: Ask Durham for a heading-and-page crosswalk to current classes and sub-items.
Keep each distinct physical bill, roll, slip, or framed object separate when identified.

open_questions.question: How should exact item manifests' CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 licence be reconciled with the catalog's separate advance-permission requirement for any published use?
next_steps: Obtain written clarification before Git publication, a public viewer, print-on-demand, commercial reuse, or adaptation.
Keep the present cache private and Git-ignored meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Why does the U-M collection browse list label volume III as 1890 while the exact item record and inspected title page say 1901?
next_steps: Report the browse-level metadata error to U-M.
Continue using the exact item and title page for the edition date.

open_questions.question: When and by what item-specific process was CME00048 first keyed before its recorded 2016 review?
next_steps: Consult U-M collection production records or archived metadata.

review_notes: Do not treat 248 editorial DIV2 units, current catalog groups, 233 digital ARKs, aggregate bill manifests, separate copies, or physical objects as interchangeable counts.

The final 233-object crosswalk excludes copy A of DCD-Bur.acs 1362-3 and copy C of DCD-Bur.acs 1374-5 because their copy-specific clauses cite only volume II; it also excludes unused Misc.Ch. 2683 and an unrelated Misc.Ch. 3118 DAO misattached under 3220.
Complete generic-label bundles mean complete provider-published inventory, not independent proof of every physical side.
